clinical_study.brief_title: Osimertinib Plus Ramucirumab vs. Osimertinib Alone in EGFR-Mutant Advanced NSCLC With CNS Involvement (RAMOSI-CNS)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Osimertinib in Combination With Ramucirumab Versus Osimertinib Monotherapy in Patients With EGFR-Mutant Stage IV Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer With Active Central Nervous System Metastases

brief_summary.textblock: This trial investigates whether the addition of ramucirumab (an anti-VEGFR2 monoclonal antibody) to osimertinib improves intracranial response and progression-free survival compared to osimertinib alone in patients with EGFR-mutant Stage IV non-small cell lung cancer harboring active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease.

detailed_description.textblock: EGFR-mutant NSCLC with CNS involvement represents a challenging clinical scenario. Osimertinib, a third-generation EGFR TKI, has demonstrated superior CNS penetration and intracranial response rates compared to earlier-generation TKIs. However, acquired resistance and CNS progression remain clinical challenges. VEGF pathway upregulation may contribute to both CNS metastasis formation and drug resistance. Ramucirumab, an anti-VEGFR2 antibody, has shown synergy with EGFR TKIs in preclinical models and in the RELAY trial (erlotinib + ramucirumab). This study tests whether adding ramucirumab to osimertinib improves intracranial disease control in EGFR-mutant NSCLC with CNS involvement. Patients are randomized 1:1 to osimertinib 80 mg/day + ramucirumab 10 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks, or osimertinib 80 mg/day alone. Imaging is performed every 6 weeks for the first 24 weeks, then every 9 weeks. Brain MRI is mandatory at baseline and every 9 weeks.

primary_outcome.measure: Intracranial Progression-Free Survival (iPFS) by RANO-BM criteria
primary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until intracranial progression or death, assessed up to 24 months

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until systemic or intracranial progression or death, up to 24 months
From randomization until death from any cause, up to 36 months
During treatment period, up to 24 months

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically or cytologically confirmed non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), adenocarcinoma histology preferred but not required - Stage IV disease (AJCC 8th edition, M1a, M1b, or M1c) - EGFR activating mutation confirmed by validated assay: exon 19 deletion or exon 21 L858R substitution (sensitizing mutations); EGFR exon 20 insertions excluded - Measurable CNS disease: at least one brain metastasis >= 5 mm on gadolinium-enhanced MRI OR documented leptomeningeal carcinomatosis - CNS metastases may be treated (post-stereotactic radiosurgery or WBRT) or untreated; if previously treated, must have at least one measurable intracranial lesion with radiographic evidence of progression or a new untreated lesion - Currently receiving or planned to initiate osimertinib 80 mg/day as first-line or second-line (post T790M) therapy; patients may be enrolled at the time of initiating osimertinib - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-2 - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1000/mm3, platelets >= 75,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 8.5 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN (or <= 5x ULN with liver metastases) - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 30 mL/min - No prior anti-VEGF/VEGFR therapy - Neurologically stable: no corticosteroid dose increase within 7 days prior to enrollment; stable or decreasing corticosteroid dose acceptable - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations - ALK- or ROS1-rearranged NSCLC - Prior treatment with osimertinib or any third-generation EGFR TKI; prior erlotinib/gefitinib/afatinib allowed - Prior anti-VEGF/VEGFR therapy (bevacizumab, ramucirumab, nintedanib) - Leptomeningeal disease requiring immediate neurosurgical intervention or high-dose corticosteroids for symptom control - Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic BP > 150 mmHg or diastolic BP > 100 mmHg despite optimal medical management) - History of arterial thromboembolic event (stroke, TIA, MI) within 6 months prior to enrollment - Active or clinically significant bleeding diathesis or coagulopathy - Interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis requiring systemic steroids within 6 months - QTcF > 470 ms at baseline or known long QT syndrome - Known hypersensitivity to osimertinib or ramucirumab - Pregnancy or breastfeeding; adequate contraception required for women of childbearing potential - Concurrent participation in another interventional clinical trial

intervention.description: Osimertinib 80 mg orally once daily continuously, plus ramucirumab 10 mg/kg intravenously every 3 weeks (21-day cycles) until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or patient withdrawal.
Osimertinib 80 mg orally once daily continuously until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or patient withdrawal.
